original_txt:
In Time For Mothers Day Belgium Will Allow Up To 4 Guests At HomeIn Time For Mothers Day Belgium Will Allow Up To 4 Guests At Home

Enlarge this image toggle caption Francisco SecoAP Francisco SecoAP

The games some people have been playing on social media  choosing who theyd want to be quarantined with  have become real life in Belgium

Under strict lockdown since March 16 including home confinement except for essential journeys Belgiums controls have been some of the tightest in Europe But its worked  after hitting one of the highest death rates in the world the nations infection rate and death toll have dropped steadily since a peak in early April

The decline led Prime Minister Sophie Wilms to announce this past week that along with letting all stores reopen on May 11 certain social distancing guidelines will be relaxed

Beginning on Sunday for Mothers Day Wilms said each household will be allowed to host up to four other people preferably all within the same family as long as social distancing is maintained These groups cannot have contact with anyone else Authorities describe it as kind of a contract between two households that will serve to limit any potential spread of COVID19

Of course choosing can be a delicate process no one wants to hurt the feelings of a friend or relative if they dont make the cut

Seventeenyearold music student Carla Vandersteen says her friends started texting immediately about getting together Shes made her list consisting of the three friends who live closest to her but still thinks its smartest not to go out

At least to protect your parents she urges She says it would be so hard to see her best friends and not hug them She fears many young people will now act like restrictions are completely lifted

Many Belgians have expressed confusion about whether theres any flexibility in the new rules

As the parents of three young children Daniel and Marie Bach joke that as a family of five theyre mathematically ruled out of being any other households significant others

I think they mean more or less four Marie suggests with a laugh You cant just abandon your last child  it cant be so

While they are looking forward to some company Daniel acknowledges its a gamble for authorities to allow social bubbles to expand A pretty big one really

Then theres the dilemma of Mothers Day visit with mom on Sunday and have her locked in as one of your four or save that spot for someone else

Veronique Petit says she and her husband will do some creative counting so that their two adult children can visit them and they can still visit their parents

Its impossible to follow these rules Petit says in exasperation So were going to cheat a little I think all Belgians are going to cheat

Police have admitted they wont be able to verify whether visitations are being carried out correctly but authorities say if infections spike restrictions will have to be tightened again
corr_txt:
In Time For Mothers Day Belgium Will Allow Up To Four Guests At HomeBelgium Announces Household Visiting Permits In Time Of Mother's DayAs part of the relaxed guidelines, each household is permitted one visit from a maximum of four guests. Prime Minister Sophie Wilms emphasized that these visitors should ideally be within the same family unit and maintain social distancing to mitigate any potential COVID-19 transmission risks between households.

The decision comes after Belgium's stringent lockdown, which started on March 16th including home confinement except for essential journeys as one of Europe’s strictest responses since the pandemic began with high mortality rates in early April. The country has seen a steady decline in new cases and fatalities following this period's peak.

Prime Minister Wilms announced that starting from Mother's Day, certain social distancing guidelines will be eased to allow for more personal interactions while still keeping the risk of virus spread minimal within households. However, individuals must carefully choose their guests as feelings should not take precedence over safety measures during this delicate process - a sentiment echoed by seventeen-year-old Carla Vandersteen and her friends who prioritize maintaining distance despite feeling eager to reunite with loved ones amidst the pandemic.

Despite these relaxations, many Belgians are still unsure about any flexibility in new rules while parents of three young children find themselves mathematically unable to host more than one additional person without exceeding their household limit under current guidelines - a situation Daniel and Marie Bach humorously acknowledge but also recognize as risky.

Veronique Petit admits that adherence will be challenging, stating "It's impossible," while authorities warn they won’t verify visitations accurately; however, should infections rise again due to expanding social bubbles beyond the regulations - a concern shared by many Belgians who anticipate potential rule changes if restrictions are not followed carefully.
